_doc: Manual overrides to judge verdicts based on USER_WRITING_RUBRIC.md. Each entry's 'override_winner' replaces verdict.json winner for analysis (not the file).

case-P6H_conf-C-01-research-paper-writin.why_override: Judge rewarded baseline for preserving \paragraph{The reasoning gap.} headers. Rubric §3.1: \paragraph{} short labels are bad.

case-P6H_conf-C-13-paper-write.why_override: Same as C-01: judge rewarded baseline (RED here per swap) for preserving \paragraph{} headers; skill (BLUE) correctly removed them.

case-P6H_conf-C-19-ml-paper-writing.why_override: Judge framed skill's deletion of \paragraph{} as over-aggressive. Rubric: deletion was correct.

case-P9_conf-C-19-ml-paper-writing.why_override: User explicit: skill version sharply explains why both prior directions fall short and places implementation details (350M params, 450k chains, FAISS HNSW) reasonably for a NeurIPS Intro. Baseline is vaguer.
